three cars qualify Safety Pick+, Institute's highest award.

Tesla Model initially earned acceptable rating small overlap test, which represents type crash that occurs when front driver-side corner vehicle hits tree utility pole collides with another vehicle.
main problem with performance Model that safety belt dummy's torso move forward, allowing dummy's head strike steering wheel hard through airbag.
Tesla made changes safety belt vehicles built after January with intent reducing dummy's forward movement.
However, when IIHS tested modified Model same problem occurred, rating didn't change.
Although tested vehicles identical structure, second test resulted greater intrusion into driver's space because left front wheel movement wasn't consistent.
Maximum intrusion increased from less than inches inches lower part inches instrument panel second test.
first test resulted good rating structural integrity, while second test resulted acceptable structural rating.
deformation limited area that didn't contain battery cells tested vehicle, this damage didn't affect rating.
Higher-performance variants Model could have battery cells this area, but, according Tesla, they also have different structure.
They haven't been tested separately aren't covered this rating.

While automatic braking comes standard, software feature only recently activated.
Before this round testing, Chevrolet Impala hadn't been through Institute's evaluations since redesigned 2014, never been rated small overlap protection.

small overlap crash, Impala's structure held reasonably well, with maximum intrusion inches lower door-hinge pillar.
